title: Granting public access to GCP resources is security-sensitive
description: Granting public (allUsers or allAuthenticatedUsers) access to GCP resources exposes them to anyone on the internet, increasing the risk of data breaches, abuse, and compliance violations. Access should be restricted to trusted users and service accounts only.
message: Resource is publicly accessible. Restrict access to trusted identities only.
impact: Publicly accessible resources can be exploited by attackers, leading to data loss, service disruption, and regulatory non-compliance.
recommendation: Remove public access (allUsers, allAuthenticatedUsers) from resource policies. Use IAM to grant access only to required principals.

noncompliant.description: Grants public access to all objects in the bucket.

compliant.description: Access is restricted to a specific trusted user.

why_is_this_an_issue: Public access to resources is a leading cause of cloud data breaches and can result in significant financial and reputational damage.
how_to_fix: Audit all resource policies. Remove any statements that allow public access. Use IAM roles and policies to enforce least privilege.

security_mappings.CERT: IDS03-J: Do not log sensitive information
